mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives corporate revenue growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The Federal Funds Rate sustained below 0.25% anchors the risk-free rate at the effective lower bound, suppressing discount rates and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% for 3+ months do not exceed baseline 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is pinned near zero while CPI inflation accelerates, deeply negative real rates trigger an inflation-scare repricing that reduces S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative during joint occurrences of FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and 3-month CPIAUCSL change > 0.5% (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, accelerating growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals healing labor income and consumption capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,000 thousand units indicates durable residential investment demand and construction sector strength, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when HOUST is above 1000 are not significantly positive compared to when HOUST is below 1000 (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment (UNRATE) and declining nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) constitute a labor market deterioration signal that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 2 months and PAYEMS falls over the same period
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint UNRATE increases and PAYEMS decreases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the CPI exceeding 1.0 percentage point signals reflationary pressure that compresses real earnings and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following 3-month CPIAUCSL changes greater than 1.0% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending momentum that lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
